answer:
The correct answer is **G. \( h \leq c \log_2(N) \)**.

In a strictly binary tree, the height \( h \) is maximized when the tree is as unbalanced as possible (a "pathological" tree), but the given condition bounds the ratio of any two root-to-leaf paths by \( c \), implying the tree cannot be too unbalanced. This restricts the height to be at most proportional to \( \log_2(N) \) with a factor \( c \), hence \( h \leq c \log_2(N) \). The other options either overstate the bound or incorrectly relate \( h \) to \( c \).
